英國唔敢正面得罪中國，所最近搵加拿大壯膽發聲明。英國自己都一片混亂，邊有能力畀咁多居留權？咪講笑啦。但你又可以諗下，點解加拿大會肯同英國發聲明，就係因為加拿大有好大既港人社區，有一定既影響力。

大家如果想務實推動平權的話，你個眼光一定唔可以放喺英國，而係要放眼整個英聯邦，尤其加拿大。以加拿大對移民承受能力，一定比英國高。香港人唔係非洲、中東難民，普遍比較富裕，而且習慣接觸西方社會，對當地影響會比較細。

冇國家可以一次過接受過百萬移民，所以 BNO 爭取既目標應該係——「英聯邦 BNO 居留權配額計劃」。計劃要分為三部份：

第一部份「落地居留簽證」
其實香港情況未危急到要即時撤走，想即時走既人未必係多數，但大家都希望有個逃生門，所以英聯邦國家可以先向 BNO 批出 Residence Visa on Arrival ，只要 BNO 入境出示呢個簽證，就可以即時獲得幾年居留權，只要搵到野做一段時間就可以入藉。

而呢個簽證只要英文達標，資產達到基本水平，甚至當地有親戚朋友擔保就可以申請，數量由全體英聯邦分擔，咁壓力就會細好多。唔好同我講點解英文要達標，要有少少錢或者擔保，你唔識英文、完全冇人冇物去到都係死路一條。

第二部份 定義何謂『聯合聲明被毀約』
銅鑼灣書店事件已經畀英國喺半年報告視為毀約，但英方係冇任何行動。既然係國際條約被毀，國際社會就應該諗法子補救，問題出於點樣既程度既毀約先可以被視為 substantial breach ，去到邊個情況英聯邦先要全面救援？英聯邦國家應該設立定期審查，由英聯邦成員決定何謂 substantial breach，一經確認，甚至連 Arrival Visa 都唔駛，直接全面接收 BNO。

第三部份 如何重建香港
幾百萬人唔係個個適應英聯邦國家生活，正如達賴啦嘛話齋，"Receive them, help them, educate them… but ultimately they should develop their own country"，重建有自主權既香港，安排政治難民回港，長遠係減輕英聯邦壓力，亦係為英聯邦重新建立亞洲據點，有益無害。英聯邦應該同美國、歐盟合作，諗下有乜辦法奪回香港，歸英又好、獨立又好。家陣只係冷戰初期，但只要冷戰進入白熱化階段，美國對香港再次產生興趣係絕對可能既，咁重建香港就會見到曙光。

大家如果想務實推動平權的話，你個眼光一定唔可以放喺英國，而係要放眼整個英聯邦，尤其加拿大。以加拿大對移民承受能力，一定比英國高。香港人唔係非洲、中東難民，普遍比較富裕，而且習慣接觸西方社會，對當地影響會比較細。

冇國家可以一次過接受過百萬移民，所以 BNO 爭取既目標應該係——「英聯邦 BNO 居留權配額計劃」。計劃要分為三部份：

第一部份「落地居留簽證」
其實香港情況未危急到要即時撤走，想即時走既人未必係多數，但大家都希望有個逃生門，所以英聯邦國家可以先向 BNO 批出 Residence Visa on Arrival ，只要 BNO 入境出示呢個簽證，就可以即時獲得幾年居留權，只要搵到野做一段時間就可以入藉。

而呢個簽證只要英文達標，資產達到基本水平，甚至當地有親戚朋友擔保就可以申請，數量由全體英聯邦分擔，咁壓力就會細好多。唔好同我講點解英文要達標，要有少少錢或者擔保，你唔識英文、完全冇人冇物去到都係死路一條。

第二部份 定義何謂『聯合聲明被毀約』
銅鑼灣書店事件已經畀英國喺半年報告視為毀約，但英方係冇任何行動。既然係國際條約被毀，國際社會就應該諗法子補救，問題出於點樣既程度既毀約先可以被視為 substantial breach ，去到邊個情況英聯邦先要全面救援？英聯邦國家應該設立定期審查，由英聯邦成員決定何謂 substantial breach，一經確認，甚至連 Arrival Visa 都唔駛，直接全面接收 BNO。

第三部份 如何重建香港
幾百萬人唔係個個適應英聯邦國家生活，正如達賴啦嘛話齋，"Receive them, help them, educate them… but ultimately they should develop their own country"，重建有自主權既香港，安排政治難民回港，長遠係減輕英聯邦壓力，亦係為英聯邦重新建立亞洲據點，有益無害。英聯邦應該同美國、歐盟合作，諗下有乜辦法奪回香港，歸英又好、獨立又好。家陣只係冷戰初期，但只要冷戰進入白熱化階段，美國對香港再次產生興趣係絕對可能既，咁重建香港就會見到曙光。

大家如果想務實推動平權的話，你個眼光一定唔可以放喺英國，而係要放眼整個英聯邦，尤其加拿大。以加拿大對移民承受能力，一定比英國高。香港人唔係非洲、中東難民，普遍比較富裕，而且習慣接觸西方社會，對當地影響會比較細。

冇國家可以一次過接受過百萬移民，所以 BNO 爭取既目標應該係——「英聯邦 BNO 居留權配額計劃」。計劃要分為三部份：

第一部份「落地居留簽證」
其實香港情況未危急到要即時撤走，想即時走既人未必係多數，但大家都希望有個逃生門，所以英聯邦國家可以先向 BNO 批出 Residence Visa on Arrival ，只要 BNO 入境出示呢個簽證，就可以即時獲得幾年居留權，只要搵到野做一段時間就可以入藉。

而呢個簽證只要英文達標，資產達到基本水平，甚至當地有親戚朋友擔保就可以申請，數量由全體英聯邦分擔，咁壓力就會細好多。唔好同我講點解英文要達標，要有少少錢或者擔保，你唔識英文、完全冇人冇物去到都係死路一條。

第二部份 定義何謂『聯合聲明被毀約』
銅鑼灣書店事件已經畀英國喺半年報告視為毀約，但英方係冇任何行動。既然係國際條約被毀，國際社會就應該諗法子補救，問題出於點樣既程度既毀約先可以被視為 substantial breach ，去到邊個情況英聯邦先要全面救援？英聯邦國家應該設立定期審查，由英聯邦成員決定何謂 substantial breach，一經確認，甚至連 Arrival Visa 都唔駛，直接全面接收 BNO。

第三部份 如何重建香港
幾百萬人唔係個個適應英聯邦國家生活，正如達賴啦嘛話齋，"Receive them, help them, educate them… but ultimately they should develop their own country"，重建有自主權既香港，安排政治難民回港，長遠係減輕英聯邦壓力，亦係為英聯邦重新建立亞洲據點，有益無害。英聯邦應該同美國、歐盟合作，諗下有乜辦法奪回香港，歸英又好、獨立又好。家陣只係冷戰初期，但只要冷戰進入白熱化階段，美國對香港再次產生興趣係絕對可能既，咁重建香港就會見到曙光。

replies.msg:
I don't know why you guys want to ask other Commonwealth countries, which haven't signed any agreements regarding Hong Kong and possess no any obligations to help BNO holders, to grant right of abode to BNO holders. LOL.. It is impossible.

The two rationales for Britain's refusal to grant right of abode in UK to BNO holders are (a.) Sino-British Joint Declaration stipulating that BNO holders must not be granted right of abode in UK without any restrictions or conditions, and (b.) the fact that it is a very serious diplomatic action denying the Sino-British Joint Declaration, and thus deteriorating Sino-British relations as a consequence.

Under these two circumstances, the possible solution to such a predicament is to ask the British government to lower the threshold for applying for BC passport so that BNO holders can be granted right of abode more easily(eg. If you have a Bachelor's degree, then you can apply for a BC passport OR if you live in UK for 2 years, you can also apply for a BC passport); such a solution can avoid any legal and constitutional controversy, and it is more acceptable for the British government as well as politicians in the House of Commons

replies.msg:
In the views of the PRC government, The Joint Declaration was expired on 1/7/1997, after the handover. It would be laughable for them to suddenly protest that the British violating a legal contract that they considered no longer in effect.

And the British government already violated it in this respect back in 1990 with the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 known as British Nationality Selection Scheme (aka 居英權計劃) The PRC government did protested and refused to admit those British Citizens. But then there was no further action.

As I said, simply granting Right of Abode/British Citizenship to BNO holders unconditionally is absolutely not practical nor favourable to the UK. The whole problem is more a UK internal affairs rather than a diplomatic issue.

In the views of the PRC government, The Joint Declaration was expired on 1/7/1997, after the handover. It would be laughable for them to suddenly protest that the British violating a legal contract that they considered no longer in effect.

And the British government already violated it in this respect back in 1990 with the British Nationality (Hong Kong) Act 1990 known as British Nationality Selection Scheme (aka 居英權計劃) The PRC government did protested and refused to admit those British Citizens. But then there was no further action.

As I said, simply granting Right of Abode/British Citizenship to BNO holders unconditionally is absolutely not practical nor favourable to the UK. The whole problem is more a UK internal affairs rather than a diplomatic issue.

Really? granting the right of abode to BNO holders without any conditions is a very clear diplomatic signal that the United Kingdom admits the fact that China is no longer willing to comply with any international treaties and the Basic Law constitutionally and legally ensuring "One Country Two System". Such a diplomatic predicament is very similar to Germany deciding to grant political asylum to Ray Wong and Alan Li in that such a Germany's decision implicitly suspects Hong Kong's judicial independence as well as China's implementation of One Country Two System; that's why Germany asked Ray Wong and Alan Li to be "low-profile" when it comes to their application for the political asylum. Particularly, Sino-British Joint Declaration, an international treaty, stipulates that BNO holders must not be granted the right of abode without any restrictions and conditions. Hence, this implies the fact that Britain denies Sino-Joint Declaration, if the status of BNO holders is unilaterally changed without any conditions.
You said that "The whole problem is more a UK internal affairs". However, there are actually many cases demonstrating the fact that domestic consideration is not as important as you guys think; the British government granted right of abode in UK to various people on the humanitarian (or you can say "obligatory") ground which prevails the domestic consideration. Eg. Prior to 1997, UK parliament passed the bill regarding granting full British Citizenship to the group of HK people who would lose their nationality after 1997. Apart from this, in 2009, the British government also decided that the BNO holders who don't possess other nationality could also be granted full British Citizenship. Those cases didn't trigger many voices of dissidents. So I don't actually think that UK internal affairs OR domestic consideration prevails diplomatic consideration.

So back to the case, I want to reiterate that there is only one possible solution available: Ask the British government to lower the threshold for applying for a BC passport so that BNO holders can possess British Citizenship more easily. Insofar as British diplomatic predicament resulting from Brexit as concern, I don't think Britain will grant BC / Right of Abode in UK without any conditions to BNO holders. In other words, UK will unconditionally grant BC/ the Right of Abode BNO holders under three main conditions: (a.) Brexit deal; Hard Brexit could potentially or may enable Britain to be more diplomatically closer to China. (b.) whole International society denies or suspects "One Country Two System", and (c.) US-China relations. As long as these three conditions are not met, BNO holders would likely never be unconditionally granted right of abode in UK

冇國家可以一次過接受過百萬移民，所以 BNO 爭取既目標應該係——「英聯邦 BNO 居留權配額計劃」。計劃要分為三部份：

第一部份「落地居留簽證」
其實香港情況未危急到要即時撤走，想即時走既人未必係多數，但大家都希望有個逃生門，所以英聯邦國家可以先向 BNO 批出 Residence Visa on Arrival ，只要 BNO 入境出示呢個簽證，就可以即時獲得幾年居留權，只要搵到野做一段時間就可以入藉。

而呢個簽證只要英文達標，資產達到基本水平，甚至當地有親戚朋友擔保就可以申請，數量由全體英聯邦分擔，咁壓力就會細好多。唔好同我講點解英文要達標，要有少少錢或者擔保，你唔識英文、完全冇人冇物去到都係死路一條。

第二部份 定義何謂『聯合聲明被毀約』
銅鑼灣書店事件已經畀英國喺半年報告視為毀約，但英方係冇任何行動。既然係國際條約被毀，國際社會就應該諗法子補救，問題出於點樣既程度既毀約先可以被視為 substantial breach ，去到邊個情況英聯邦先要全面救援？英聯邦國家應該設立定期審查，由英聯邦成員決定何謂 substantial breach，一經確認，甚至連 Arrival Visa 都唔駛，直接全面接收 BNO。

第三部份 如何重建香港
幾百萬人唔係個個適應英聯邦國家生活，正如達賴啦嘛話齋，"Receive them, help them, educate them… but ultimately they should develop their own country"，重建有自主權既香港，安排政治難民回港，長遠係減輕英聯邦壓力，亦係為英聯邦重新建立亞洲據點，有益無害。英聯邦應該同美國、歐盟合作，諗下有乜辦法奪回香港，歸英又好、獨立又好。家陣只係冷戰初期，但只要冷戰進入白熱化階段，美國對香港再次產生興趣係絕對可能既，咁重建香港就會見到曙光。

I don't know why you guys want to ask other Commonwealth countries, which haven't signed any agreements regarding Hong Kong and possess no any obligations to help BNO holders, to grant right of abode to BNO holders. LOL.. It is impossible.

The two rationales for Britain's refusal to grant right of abode in UK to BNO holders are (a.) Sino-British Joint Declaration stipulating that BNO holders must not be granted right of abode in UK without any restrictions or conditions, and (b.) the fact that it is a very serious diplomatic action denying the Sino-British Joint Declaration, and thus deteriorating Sino-British relations as a consequence.

Under these two circumstances, the possible solution to such a predicament is to ask the British government to lower the threshold for applying for BC passport so that BNO holders can be granted right of abode more easily(eg. If you have a Bachelor's degree, then you can apply for a BC passport OR if you live in UK for 2 years, you can also apply for a BC passport); such a solution can avoid any legal and constitutional controversy, and it is more acceptable for the British government as well as politicians in the House of Commons

In fact I am not the first guy to ask Commonwealth for help. I have heard Hong Kong Watch and some BNO communities is working on it.

I don't expect Commonwealth will unconditionally let BNOs in. There may have some restrictions and requirements, but IT IS POSSIBLE.

UK had promised not to grant rights of adobe to BNOs directly but they may offer a "tailor-made" immigration program for BNOs.
